codeReview.summary: Implementation is thorough and covers all required behavior: token icons in From/To inputs with proper lifecycle (show on select, hide on clear, update on change, onerror fallback, 375px safe). CSS uses clean wrapper/no-icon pattern. All 8 code paths where tokens are set or cleared have corresponding icon updates (selectToken, applyDefaults, handleTokenInputBlur, handleTokenSwapIfNeeded, URL param restoration, unrecognized token save, input clear in refresh). 109 insertions, 4 deletions in a single file. All tests pass (136), typecheck and lint clean.

issues.description: Variable shadowing: top-level `const fromIcon = document.getElementById('fromIcon')` and `const toIcon = document.getElementById('toIcon')` are shadowed by local `const fromIcon = renderResultTokenIcon(...)` / `const toIcon = renderResultTokenIcon(...)` inside renderSpandexResult (~line 4999) and renderCurveResult (~line 5079). No functional bug since the locals are HTML strings used only within their render functions, but the identical names could confuse future maintainers.

Stale icon when typing over selection: The refresh() function only clears the icon when `!input.value.trim()`. If a user types a new address over a previously selected token (e.g., USDC icon showing, user types a raw 0x address), the old icon persists until input is fully cleared, a new token is selected from autocomplete, or blur triggers handleTokenInputBlur. Minor UX gap — icon shows stale token during the typing-to-blur transition.

Initial `src=""` on img elements: Some browsers interpret an empty src attribute as a request to the page URL. The `.no-icon` class applies `display: none` which should prevent rendering, but omitting src entirely (or using a data URI placeholder) would be more defensive.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The token input icon pattern introduces a new UI component pattern (wrapper div + positioned img + no-icon toggle class) that's distinct from both the modal pattern and the autocomplete-logo pattern. This could be documented in the library for consistency in future icon-in-input implementations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/server.ts lines 1841-1866: .token-input-wrapper, .token-input-icon, .no-icon pattern. Handoff documents the pattern but it's not captured in .factory/library/.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker reused the variable names fromIcon/toIcon for DOM refs at global scope, which shadows pre-existing local const declarations of the same name inside render functions. AGENTS.md and the skill don't mention avoiding variable shadowing in the inline JS template, though this is a general best practice.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Line 2391: `const fromIcon = document.getElementById('fromIcon')` shadows line ~4999: `const fromIcon = renderResultTokenIcon(data.from, quoteChainId)` inside renderSpandexResult. Same for toIcon.

summary: Feature passes review. The implementation correctly adds token icons to From/To input fields with comprehensive lifecycle management across all 8 code paths that set or clear tokens. CSS is clean and follows the existing pattern structure. Three non-blocking issues identified: variable shadowing of fromIcon/toIcon by pre-existing render functions, stale icon during type-over scenarios, and empty src attribute on initial img tags. None are functional bugs — all expectedBehavior items (VAL-UI-010 through VAL-UI-015) are fulfilled. Worker followed the fullstack-worker skill procedure correctly, ran all verification commands, and performed browser testing at both desktop and 375px widths.
